title: Import Export Specialist
description: An Import Export Specialist in India spends most of their time coordinating with suppliers and buyers, ensuring compliance with regulations, and managing logistics. They also handle documentation, customs clearance, and negotiations with clients. Their work involves a mix of administrative tasks, communication, and problem-solving, often under tight deadlines and with limited resources.
degree_name: Bachelor of Commerce in International Business
top_govt_colleges: Lady Shri Ram College for Women
top_private_colleges: SP Jain Institute of Management and Research
Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies
Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology

career_progression.year_0_2: Assistants in import-export companies, handling documentation and coordination tasks.
career_progression.year_3_5: Team leads in import-export companies, overseeing logistics and negotiations.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior managers in import-export companies, handling strategic planning and decision-making.
senior_paths: Senior manager in a multinational company
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Start a logistics or supply chain management company, or offer consulting services to import-export businesses.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities for working in international trade and logistics with companies in the US, EU, or Asia.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹4-6 lakhs per annum, with a steep learning curve and long working hours.
common_misconceptions: Import-export is a lucrative career with high salaries
It's easy to start a career in import-export with minimal experience
